intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments respond better to friendly, playful voice than clinical/serious tone
facade.axisTargeted: friendly vs professional, playful vs serious
facade.label: Casual tone, light humor
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard copy, error messages, and empty states should feel conversational and warm
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should maintain professional, measured tone throughout

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments respond better to friendly, playful voice than clinical/serious tone
evidence.implication: Dashboard copy, error messages, and empty states should feel conversational and warm

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid transitions when data updates or panels open, rather than instant appearance
facade.axisTargeted: MOTION AND BEHAVIOR — Animated transitions vs instant changes
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard benefits from easing, fade-ins, and choreographed micro-interactions as experiments evolve and new data arrives
facade.rejectImplies: Users want snappy, instant feedback with no animation delay — data appears immediately without transition flourish

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer gentle, approachable visual language over clinical sharpness
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should feel inviting and human-centered, using curves and soft edges to reduce anxiety around data entry and health tracking
facade.rejectImplies: Users want a more professional, precise, no-nonsense interface—sharp lines and corners signal competence and accuracy in health monitoring

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar organizing experiment categories (weight, sleep, mood, etc.) with the main content area showing current metrics and data — vs. top tabs that reset on scroll
facade.label: Sidebar nav
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick category switching and wants experiments grouped visually apart from detail view; sidebar becomes primary wayfinding
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer flat hierarchy; tabs or other navigation model may suit experiments better, or dashboard shouldn't hide categories on scroll

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Personal health dashboards work better with time-boxed summary cards (one metric + trend per card) rather than comprehensive tables or live-updating streams
facade.label: Daily snapshot cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick glanceability and habit-stacking; prefers card-based layouts with 1–3 data points per card; dashboard should surface only the most actionable metrics daily
facade.rejectImplies: User wants comprehensive context in single view; prefers tables, full datasets, or narrative summaries over micro-metrics; may need drill-down access to detailed logs rather than pre-filtered daily highlights

facade.hypothesis: A personal health dashboard works best with restrained, understated data visualization rather than colorful or animated graphs
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs digital — whether the interface feels natural and accessible or stark and clinical
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers calm, unfussy data presentation; likely values clarity over visual interest; may want a contemplative, uncluttered mood
facade.rejectImplies: User wants more visual richness, color, or visual feedback in charts; may prefer warmer, more engaging presentation of data; could indicate appetite for more organic or energetic mood

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer encouraging, celebratory language over neutral reporting when logging data and viewing results
facade.label: Motivational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Copy should celebrate milestones, use positive reinforcement, frame setbacks as learning opportunities
facade.rejectImplies: Copy should remain neutral and factual, avoiding emotional language or gamification elements
